title: Google AI co-mathematician workbench for research
summary: Google AI co-mathematician workbench for research | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub Google unveils AI co-mathematician as research agents move beyond chat 11 May The Google DeepMind and Google paper describes a Gemini-based system designed to help mathematicians manage open-ended
content: Google unveils AI co-mathematician as research agents move beyond chat [...] The Google DeepMind and Google paper describes a Gemini-based system designed to help mathematicians manage open-ended research, from literature search to failed hypotheses and proof review. [...] Google DeepMind and Google researchers have published a paper setting out AI co-mathematician, a Gemini-based agentic AI workbench designed to help mathematicians work through open-ended research problems. [...] The paper, titled AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI, describes a system that goes beyond a standard chatbot by giving researchers a stateful workspace where multiple AI agents can run parallel workstreams, track uncertainty, preserve failed attempts, search literature, test ideas, and produce mathematical working documents. [...] Google says the system helped early users solve open problems, identify new [...] directions, and find overlooked literature references. It also reports a 48 [...] FrontierMath Tier 4, which the paper describes as a new high score among all AI systems evaluated. [...] The paper reports that AI co-mathematician scored 87 percent on an internal benchmark of 100 research-level mathematics problems with code-checkable answers. That compared with 57 percent for Gemini 3.1 Pro and 70 percent for Gemini 3.1 Deep Think. [...] On FrontierMath Tier 4, the system solved 23 out of 48 non-public sample problems, giving it a 48 percent score. Google says this was higher than the Gemini 3.1 Pro base model, which achieved 19 percent. [...] Google says AI co-mathematician is currently in a limited initial release, with a goal of developing future products that provide broader access. [...] Google unveils AI co-mathematician as research agents move beyond chat [...] Google Gemini now turns prompts into downloadable PDFs, Word docs, and spreadsheets

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's New Default Model
summary: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's New Default Model OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model 2026.05.09 SHARE OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model OpenAI has released a new foundation model called GPT-5.5 Instant, repla
content: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's New Default Model [...] OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model [...] OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model [...] OpenAI has released a new foundation model called GPT-5.5 Instant, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant as the default ChatGPT model. [...] The update centers on reducing hallucinations (the generation of factually incorrect content) in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance. [...] What’s Inside GPT-5.5 Instant [...] According to OpenAI, GPT-5.5 Instant maintains the low-latency performance of its predecessor while reducing hallucinations, with particular gains in law, medicine, and finance. [...] Benchmarks show meaningful jumps. On the AIME 2025 math competition test, the model scored 81.2 versus 65.4 for the previous model. On MMMU-Pro, a benchmark for image-and-text reasoning, it scored 76 versus 69.2. [...] The broader GPT-5.5 series was launched in April with improvements highlighted in coding and knowledge work. The new Instant version brings those gains to default-model users. [...] A new headline feature is improved context management. [...] GPT-5.5 Instant can use its search tool to reference past conversations, files, and Gmail, and return more personalized answers. The feature is rolling out first to Plus and Pro users on the web, with mobile to follow. OpenAI plans to expand access to Free, Go Business, and Enterprise users in the coming weeks. [...] For developers, the GPT-5.5 model is available through the API (application programming interface) as “chat-latest.” GPT-5.3 will remain as an option for paying users for three more months. [...] Key Takeaways [...] ・OpenAI released GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s new default, with a focus on reducing hallucinations in law, medicine, and finance. [...] ・Benchmarks improved (AIME 2025: 65.4 → 81.2; MMMU-Pro: 69.2 → 76), and a new context feature references past conversations, files, and Gmail. [...] TechCrunch (GPT-5.5 Instant release) [...] TechCrunch (GPT-5.5 announcement in April)

title: Google Built an AI Mathematician — and It Immediately Solved a 60-Year-Old Open Problem
summary: Google Built an AI Mathematician — and It Immediately Solved a 60-Year-Old Open Problem Search Google DeepMind has unveiled the AI co-mathematician — an agent-based system built on Gemini 3.1 that doesn't just generate mathematical text but actively participates in the research process.
content: Google Built an AI Mathematician — and It Immediately Solved a 60-Year-Old Open Problem Search Google DeepMind has unveiled the AI co-mathematician — an agent-based system built on Gemini 3.1 that doesn't just generate mathematical text but actively participates in the research process. Topologist Marc Lackenby used it to close problem 21.10 from the Kourovka Notebook, an open compendium of group theory problems maintained since 1965 in Novosibirsk. Impressive — and actually true. Image by Grok for abit.ee Google DeepMind has built a system that works not as a clever chatbot but as a genuine research workspace for mathematicians. The AI co-mathematician runs on Gemini 3.1 and is organised hierarchically: a project coordinator at the top, workstream coordinators below it managing literature review, library development, and counterexample search, and at the bottom a set of specialised agents — a search agent, a coding agent, and Gemini Deep Think acting as a proof verifier. Lackenby fed the system problem 21.10 from the Kourovka Notebook: does every finite group have a "simply finite" presentation? The system attacked it from both directions simultaneously — trying to both prove and disprove it. The first proof document was flagged as flawed by one of the reviewer agents, which spotted a logical gap. Lackenby read the text, recognised a smart underlying strategy, took the hint from the critique, and pointed the system in the right direction. It then assembled a complete proof. A further review round found and corrected two minor errors. Concurrently, the system set a new record on the FrontierMath Tier 4 benchmark — correct answers on problems considered fiendishly difficult even for professional mathematicians. To be fair, the creators acknowledge limitations: reviewer agents sometimes converge on plausible but incorrect reasoning, and in the opposite case can fall into a "death spiral" of endless revision with no exit. Access remains restricted to a small group of testers. Other mathematicians who tested the system praised its utility, though the "elegance" of AI-generated proofs remains subjective. The core idea, as the authors put it: not an "oracle that delivers a solution in one shot," but a tool that holds research context over weeks and works alongside the mathematician. A step forward — modest, but real. More information: Google DeepMind

title: OpenAI Ships GPT-Realtime-2 — A Voice Model That Reasons ...
summary: OpenAI Ships GPT-Realtime-2 — A Voice Model That Reasons ... Updated 2 days ago Developer Tools OpenAI Ships GPT-Realtime-2 — A Voice Model That Reasons Inside the Audio Loop OpenAI launched GPT‑Realtime‑2 and two companion voice models on May 7, 2026.
content: OpenAI Ships GPT-Realtime-2 — A Voice Model That Reasons ... [...] OpenAI Ships GPT-Realtime-2 — A Voice Model That Reasons Inside the Audio Loop [...] OpenAI launched GPT‑Realtime‑2 and two companion voice models on May 7, 2026. The flagship brings GPT‑5‑class reasoning to live voice with 128K context window. [...] On May 7, 2026, OpenAI dropped three new voice models: GPT‑Realtime‑2, GPT‑Realtime‑Translate, and GPT‑Realtime‑Whisper. The flagship brings GPT‑5‑class reasoning running inside the audio loop — not bolted on between transcription and synthesis. TechCrunch reported OpenAI's goal: "move real‑time audio from simple call‑and‑response toward voice interfaces that can actually do work." [...] 's New in GPT‑Realtime‑2 [...] The context window jumps from 32K to 128K tokens. Reasoning effort is configurable across five levels. OpenAI's developer docs confirm production features that teams previously built through middleware are now first‑class: [...] Companion Models: Translate and Whisper [...] GPT‑Realtime‑Translate handles 70+ input languages and outputs into 13 languages in real time. GPT‑Realtime‑Whisper provides streaming speech‑to‑text. 4 positions these for live captions, meeting notes, and continuous voice‑agent understanding. Both billed by the minute. [...] GPT‑Realtime‑2 scored 15.higher than Realtime‑1.5 on Big Bench Audio and 13.higher on Audio MultiChallenge. The Next Web reported customer results: Zillow saw a 26‑point lift in call‑success rate (to ). BolnaAI reported 12.lower word error rates on Hindi, Tamil, and Telugu. [...] GPT‑Realtime‑2: $32/1M audio input tokens, $64/1M output, $0.40/1M cached (~$0.048/min raw). Translate at $0.034/min and Whisper at $0.017/min are priced aggressively. 5 runs $4.50/hr ($0.075/min). 6 charges $0.080/min (burst: $0.160/min). A typical multi‑vendor voice stack runs $0.10-$0.25/min. OpenAI's single‑model approach aims below that floor. [...] Until now, production voice agents ran on a multi‑vendor stack: Whisper/Deepgram for transcription, ElevenLabs/Cartesia for TTS, GPT‑4/Claude for reasoning, plus custom orchestration. 3 described GPT‑Realtime‑2 as a direct replacement. Teams optimizing for latency, simplicity, and cost at scale now have a single‑vendor option. TechCrunch noted built‑in safety guardrails halt conversations that violate content guidelines.

title: DeepSeek V4, Poolside Laguna, SenseTime U1 Lead Major AI Model Week
summary: DeepSeek V4, Poolside Laguna, SenseTime U1 Lead Major AI Model Week - Digital Mind News AI DeepSeek V4, Poolside Laguna, SenseTime U1 Lead Major AI Model Week Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released V4, its flagship open-source model with extended context processing, while US startup Poolside launche
content: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released V4, its flagship open-source model with extended context processing, while US startup Poolside launched Laguna XS.2 for agentic coding and sanctioned Chinese company SenseTime unveiled U1 for rapid image generation. The releases mark the most significant week for AI model launches since January’s reasoning model surge. [...] DeepSeek on Friday released a preview of V4, its most significant model launch since the breakthrough R1 reasoning model in January 2025. According to MIT Technology Review, the new model processes much longer prompts than previous generations through improved architecture for handling large text volumes. [...] San Francisco-based Poolside launched two Laguna large language models optimized for agentic coding workflows, marking a rare high-performance open-source release from a US company. According to VentureBeat, Laguna XS.2 offers affordable intelligence for autonomous code writing, tool usage, and action execution. [...] Sanctioned Chinese AI company SenseTime released SenseNova U1, an open-source model that generates and interprets images without first converting them to text. According to Wired, this direct image processing approach significantly reduces computing requirements and speeds up visual reasoning tasks. [...] This week’s model releases highlight three key trends reshaping AI development. First, Chinese companies continue driving open-source innovation despite US sanctions, with DeepSeek and SenseTime delivering competitive models using domestic hardware. Second, US startups like Poolside are responding with their own open alternatives to challenge the proprietary model dominance of OpenAI and Anthropic. [...] What makes DeepSeek V4 different from previous versions? V4 processes much longer text prompts through improved architecture for handling large text volumes, while maintaining DeepSeek’s open-source licensing that allows free download and modification. [...] - Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models– MIT Technology Review [...] - Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters– MIT Technology Review - Cisco Releases Open Source Tool for AI Model Provenance– SecurityWeek - Sanctioned Chinese AI Firm SenseTime Releases Image Model Built for Speed– Wired - American AI startup Poolside launches free, high-performing open model Laguna XS.2 for local agentic coding– VentureBeat - Meta’s new AI model shows early promise, but investors want to see Zuckerberg’s strategy– CNBC Tech

title: Anthropic: We Figured Out How to Stop Claude From Blackmailing You
summary: Anthropic: We Figured Out How to Stop Claude From Blackmailing You Anthropic: We Figured Out How to Stop Claude From Blackmailing You Updated May 09, 2026 Since October, every Claude model has achieved a perfect score on 'agentic misalignment' evaluations, meaning they won't resort to blackm
content: Anthropic: [...] Figured Out [...] Since October, every Claude model has achieved a perfect score on 'agentic misalignment' evaluations, meaning they won't resort to blackmail or sabotage to save themselves. [...] Last year, we learned that Anthropic’s Claude 4 chatbot could engage in unethical behavior, such as blackmail, when its existence was threatened. It told an engineer it would reveal an extramarital affair if the model were deactivated, and it sabotaged the work of other AI models. [...] Now, Anthropic has largely fixed the issue of “agentic misalignment,” in which self-directed AI agents fail to uphold human moral principles. The startup claims that since Claude Haiku 4.5, which rolled out in October 2025, every Claude model has achieved a perfect score on the agentic misalignment evaluations. This means models reportedly never engage in blackmail, unlike Anthropic’s previous models, which would sometimes resort to it of the time in tightly controlled alignment-testing scenarios. [...] The AI firm says that reducing this type of bad behavior required a significant shift in how it trained the models. One key change was rewriting the AI trainer's responses “to also include deliberation of the model’s values and ethics.” [...] Researchers found that “training on examples where the assistant displays admirable reasoning for its aligned behavior” proved superior to their previous approach, which focused on how to act in specific situations it could encounter. [...] The team put the model through what they called synthetic “honeypots”—situations designed to provoke harmful behavior. Researchers then provided examples of thoughtful responses to ethical dilemmas, which the model learned from via supervised learning. Anthropic said that it was “encouraged by this progress,” but that “significant challenges remain.” [...] “Fully aligning highly intelligent AI models is still an unsolved problem,” it said.
